heading: Configure Replica Set Scaling Mode
body:
Modify the [replica set](manual/reference/glossary) scaling mode for your
cluster. Atlas uses the scaling mode **In Parallel By Workload Type**
by default. Atlas can also scale a replica set with the **In Parallel
By Node Type** and **Sequential** modes.

The following list describes the available scaling modes:

- **In Parallel By Workload Type** mode applies only to clusters
  with read-only [operational node](manual/reference/glossary) and [analytics node](manual/reference/glossary).
  Atlas uses this scaling mode by default, unless you specify another
  scaling mode. In this mode, Atlas scales your analytics nodes in
  parallel with your operational nodes.

  > **Note:** If your cluster has only [electable](manual/reference/glossary)
  > nodes, the scaling mode **In Parallel By Workload Type**
  > doesn't affect cluster behavior.

- **In Parallel By Node Type** mode is for large, dynamic
  workloads requiring frequent and timely cluster tier scaling. In
  this mode, Atlas scales your [electable](manual/reference/glossary)
  nodes in parallel with your [read-only nodes](manual/reference/glossary) and
  [analytics node](manual/reference/glossary) nodes. This is the fastest scaling
  strategy, but it might impact latency of workloads when performing
  extensive secondary reads.

- **Sequential** mode is for steady-state workloads and applications
  performing latency-sensitive secondary reads. In this mode, Atlas
  scales all nodes sequentially.
